name: Tickets (TIX)
description: Removed on April 14, 2016, Tickets were another currency, alongside Robux, that could be earned by players through visiting Roblox daily or other players joining your games. Like Robux, Tickets could be spent on Marketplace items and user advertisements.

name: Guests
description: Before being removed on October 2, 2017, people who wanted to play Roblox but didn't want to create an account could play as a "Guest" account. Guest accounts had limited access to features and could not chat or spend Robux.

name: All Things Roblox
description: All Things Roblox was one of the four Roblox section subforums.

name: Builders Club Avatar Items
description: Removed in April 2019, specific items on the Avatar Marketplace could only be purchased by players who were part of the Builders Club membership. All users who originally obtained these items kept them after they went offsale.

name: Bevels
description: Bevels are a partially discontinued graphical Roblox feature, which rendered smooth, 45-degree edges on meshless Parts.

name: Classic Heads
description: Removed on March 24, 2026, all Classic Heads and Faces were converted to Dynamic Heads with animated faces.

name: Faces
description: Removed on March 24, 2026, all Classic Heads and Faces were converted to Dynamic Heads with animated faces.

name: Comments
description: Prior to 2016, users could leave comments under various assets, including avatar items, passes, badges, and games. On May 16, 2016, comments were removed from avatar items, while on December 2, 2016, comments were removed from games.

name: Video Star Codes
description: Removed on October 24, 2025, Video Star Codes could be entered by any user when purchasing Robux to give members of the Star Video Program 5% back in revenue.

name: Community Walls
description: Removed on March 21, 2026, Community Walls were a place for Community members to post comments in a feed-style chain. This feature was replaced by Community Forums.

name: Player Points
description: Discontinued in 2018, Player Points were earned by players in different games to showcase a players skill in that game and display on the Leaderboard, which was also later removed. Player Points still existed in the backend until November 2023.

name: Xbox One S Roblox Bundle
description: The Xbox One S Roblox Bundle was a Xbox One S bundle created by Microsoft for the Xbox One S. Purchasing the bundle granted exclusive Rthro avatar bundles, avatar accessories, and Robux.

name: Builders Club
description: Discontinued on September 23, 2019, Builders Club was the predecessor to the current Roblox Premium subscription. Depending on the tier, Builders Club members received a daily Robux stipend, additional Group slots, exclusive badges, and more.

name: Roblox Premium
description: Roblox Premium is an outgoing paid membership that gives users perks and benefits.

name: Group Clans
description: Removed on January 23, 2019, Clans were used by Groups to showcase Player Points earned on a game's Leaderboard. With the discontinuation of Player Points and Leaderboards respectively, this feature became obsolete.

name: Ambassador program
description: The Ambassador Program acted as a Roblox referral system members to gain more Tickets.

name: Engagement-Based Payouts
description: Engagement-Based Payouts (formerly Premium Payout) was a feature which allow developers to be paid in Robux based on how much time Premium subscribers spend in your game.

name: Forums
description: Removed on December 11, 2017, Forums were a place where players could talk and communicate with each other in a text-based format. Each category had sub-forums that could be used to discuss various topics relating to Roblox.

name: Luobu
description: Luobu was the mainland Chinese version of Roblox.

name: Guilded
description: Guilded was a VoIP, instant messaging and digital distribution platform developed by Guilded Inc. and owned by Roblox Corporation.

name: Survive the Disasters
description: Survive the Disasters was an action video game published by Roblox Corporation. It simulated Stickmasterluke's Natural Disaster Survival experience.

name: Hang Out in a Disco and Chat
description: Hang Out in a Disco and Chat is a discontinued iOS app developed by Roblox Corporation.

name: Roblox Developer
description: The Roblox Developer App was a mobile app released on the iOS App Store. The app allowed the user to pair their device to Roblox Studio so that they could use it as a testing device when using Server-Client mode.

name: Roblox Developer Hub
description: The Roblox Developer Hub was an official resource about Roblox development. It gathers tutorials and guides on game development and has an API reference with documentation on classes, enums, and the global namespace.

name: Roblox Developer Wiki
description: The Roblox Developer Wiki was the official resource about Roblox development. It gathered tutorials and guides on game development and had an API reference with documentation on classes and the global namespace.

name: Toys
description: Roblox toys were physical real life small collectible mini-figures and playsets based on various notable characters, users, experiences and other elements of the Roblox universe.

name: System Badges
description: Removed on February 9, 2026, System Badges were displayed on profiles and showcased achievements earned on the Roblox site, including visit milestones, friendships, and combat scripts which were retired in 2015.

name: 13+ Avatar Items
description: Removed on July 3, 2020, specific items on the Avatar Marketplace could only be purchased by players who were 13 years of age or older. After the removal, all items with the 13+ tag could be purchased by any user regardless of age.

name: Gift Accessories
description: Discontinued in 2018, Gift accessories were an avatar item that would "unwrap" or turn into a different avatar item, like a gift. Users would purchase the gift not knowing what would come out of it until it was opened.
